title: campnosperma coriaceum
intro: Campnosperma coriaceum, also known as the Tamanu tree, is a species of tree in the family Sapindaceae native to tropical Africa. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and infections, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Campnosperma coriaceum include wound healing, skin regeneration, and anti-aging effects, as well as potential anti-cancer properties. Herbal preparations of Campnosperma coriaceum include oil and extract, which can be applied topically to the skin or taken internally in moderation, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential interactions.
